original_reasoning: Thanatos vocative=Death; thnt=stink/decay. Semantic link: death/decay. Possible shared IE/Semitic root th-n-t.

Arabic sa'at = the two sides of the throat/windpipe (anatomical, upper torso). Greek stethea = nom/acc.pl of stethos 'chests, breasts'. Both denote upper-torso anatomical regions. Strong concrete link with phonetic resemblance sa-t / steth.
ἰχθυόρροος means swarming with fish; خثر includes mixedness and thick remainder, a plausible crowding concept.
Abundance/kathura (many) vs 'analogical/extended use' - shared excess/over-use notion
Unexplained root (Ibn Jinni) vs Cerberus - phonetic match but uncertain meaning link
Arabic fazz = 'restless, startled, fleeing'; Greek phyza = 'panicked rout from battle'. Plausible semantic overlap of panic-flight.
Greek 'delicate/light' can align with a fine crafted object; a plausible abstracted quality shift.

الصقب = ولد الناقة (offspring of a she-camel); also جمعه = gathered it. βλώσκω = to come, to go. The offspring as 'the one who comes forth' and βλώσκω as 'coming' share the concept of emergence/arrival. Birth as a form of coming is conceptually strong.
